blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: That description matches the individual who was found tonight.
Julian set his mug down on the porch rail.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Then whoever was watching knew they were looking at someone who wouldn't be there much longer.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Or they were looking at a friend. People look at their friends, Mr. Crane. Especially in a group, at a fire, at night.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: But the quality of the looking was wrong. You spend enough time watching people watch people and you learn the difference between someone who cares about a person and someone who is tracking one. Caring looks away. Caring moves around, joins the conversation, checks back in. What I saw didn't do any of that. It stayed on one point. The whole time I was in line of sight.
The night was very quiet. The ocean was a constant underneath everything.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: You understand this is a significant thing to say.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I'm not implying anything. I'm telling you what I observed. What it means is your job.
Davenport looked at him for a long moment. Then at the house next door. Then he noticed he'd taken his hat off again without realizing it.
